methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Occam is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Ana Montero Horas and Andre Marques-Smith that has raised $14M to date. The target operates within Biotech & Health, a sector represented by 3 public comps (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Chai Discovery.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression proved applicable. This method uses total capital raised ($14M) as a proxy for post-money value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A step-ups, implying a valuation of $35M. Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the biotech universe—while 8 private peers exist in the sector, none satisfied narrow filtering criteria. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median: 6.9x across 3 tickers) could not be executed because Occam's revenue is not on file, a typical constraint for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $30M–$40M, centered on $35M. This range reflects a single methodological anchor and should be treated as indicative rather than definitive. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer revenue multiples, limited visibility into Occam's own financial performance, and the broad dispersion typical of early-stage biotech valuations. Additional financial disclosure or closer peer comparability would materially refine this estimate.
